behavior_summary: U8P_GASP::hatch runs as a short process to handle an egg 'hatching' event: it obtains an egg identifier from the current item, invokes an item-level guardian routine (Item::guardianBark) passing the egg id and an out-address to capture any returned value, then destroys the egg instance via Item::destroy.

The guardian-related call (Item::guardianBark) is executed as a process and appears to produce an out-value (captured into a local), suggesting it either spawns/configures a guardian entity or returns a status/ID used by higher-level systems.
After invoking guardian logic, the egg item is removed from the world (cleanup) to complete the U8P_GASP::hatch lifecycle.
notable_control_flow: Linear, single-path sequence: call Egg::getEggId(this) -> call Item::guardianBark(eggId, out_addr) -> call Item::destroy(this).
No branching, menus, flags, or player-facing dialogue are present; the function is a utility/process-style side-effect handler.
All effects appear local to the item instance and external procedures called; state changes are performed via external calls rather than global flags.
open_questions: Does Item::guardianBark spawn an NPC guardian, only play a bark (sound/text), or both? The function name and out-parameter suggest a spawn/ID return but this is inferred.
What is the exact meaning and contents of the out-value written by Item::guardianBark (captured into the local 'temp')? Is it an entity id, status code, or text index?

What event in the engine triggers this process in gameplay (explicit 'U8P_GASP::hatch' timer, use action, or other lifecycle event)? The process type (0x0210) implies an in-world process but the caller is not present in this snippet.

main_function.name: U8P_GASP::hatch
main_function.purpose: Perform the U8P_GASP::hatch side-effects for an egg item: determine egg identity, hand off guardian/spawn/bark work to the item-level guardian routine (capturing any returned data), then destroy the egg item to complete cleanup.
entry_conditions: Declared as a process (processType 0x0210) — inferred to run when the egg item reaches its U8P_GASP::hatch event or is activated in-world.
No local conditional checks or menu input; starts immediately when the process is invoked.

function_map.name: Egg::getEggId
function_map.role: return egg identifier for this item

function_map.name: Item::guardianBark
function_map.role: perform guardian-related behavior (likely spawn/configure guardian and/or play bark); writes an out-value

external_calls.purpose: Obtain an identifier for the current egg item (result stored in local1); used to drive the guardian behavior.
Invoke guardian-related logic as a process, passing the egg id and an out-address to capture a returned value (likely entity id or status).
Destroy/remove the egg item from the world after guardian logic completes (cleanup).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GASP::hatch runs as a short process to handle an egg 'hatching' event: it obtains an egg identifier from the current item, invokes an item-level guardian routine (Item::guardianBark) passing the egg id and an out-address to capture any returned value, then destroys the egg instance via Item::destroy.
